title: eucalyptus cordata subsp. quadrangulosa
intro: Eucalyptus cordata subsp. quadrangulosa, commonly known as the yellow gum or white peppermint gum, is a subspecies of the Eucalyptus cordata plant, native to Tasmania and southeastern Australia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as colds, coughs, and bronchitis. The essential oil of Eucalyptus cordata subsp. quadrangulosa has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and promote overall health and wellbeing. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, inhalers, and topical creams, which can be used to relieve respiratory issues and promote a sense of calm and relaxation.
